questions.question_text: All real estate advertising in South Dakota must include:
questions.correct_answer: The responsible broker's name as licensed
distractors: The broker associate's personal phone number
The SDREC license number of each agent
questions.explanation: South Dakota requires all advertising to include the responsible broker's name as it appears on their license. This applies to all media including print, internet, and social media. The purpose is to ensure the public knows which broker oversees the transaction.

questions.question_text: A broker associate posts a property listing on social media using only their personal name. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, the responsible broker must be identified
distractors: Yes, social media is exempt from ad rules
Yes, if the post includes the property address
No, but only if the post is a paid promotion
questions.explanation: Internet and social media advertising must comply with the same identification rules as traditional advertising. Every ad must include the responsible broker's name. A post with only the agent's name is a blind ad, which is prohibited.

questions.question_text: What is a 'blind ad' in South Dakota real estate?
distractors: An ad placed without the seller's consent
An ad that does not include the property address
An ad targeting a specific demographic group
questions.explanation: A blind ad is one that does not identify the responsible broker. Blind ads are prohibited in South Dakota because consumers must be able to identify the licensed broker behind any real estate marketing.

questions.question_text: A real estate team places an advertisement featuring only the team name and logo. Does this comply with SD advertising rules?
questions.correct_answer: No, team ads must also include the broker's identity
distractors: Yes, the team name is sufficient identification
Yes, if the team is registered with the SDREC
No, team advertising is prohibited entirely
questions.explanation: Team advertising must include the responsible broker's identity in addition to any team branding. A team name alone does not satisfy the broker identification requirement, as consumers need to know which broker supervises the team.

questions.question_text: A licensee forms a business entity under SDCL 36-21A-46.1. Which requirement still applies?
distractors: The entity replaces the individual license
The entity is exempt from advertising rules
The entity may operate without broker affiliation
questions.explanation: Even when licensees form business entities, they must still comply with responsible broker supervision requirements. The business entity structure does not override the supervisory relationship or any advertising identification obligations.
